text.tspan: if transaction T1 commits and returns to client,
and then client starts T2 that observes T1's writes,
then T2's commit timestamp will be > T1's.
Without wait: a writer in another datacenter could pick
a timestamp earlier than T1 u2014 violating ordering visible to clients.
With wait: by the time client knows T1 committed,
no clock anywhere can produce a timestamp < T1's commit_ts.

g.text: Phase 3: COMMIT-WAIT u2014 wait until clock-bound passes
Phase 4: only NOW return to client
